headlines.text: Iran War Day 98: Hezbollah rejects US-brokered ceasefire as Israel strikes continue across Lebanon
Blowout May jobs report adds 172,000 jobs — double expectations — sending stocks lower in 'good news is bad news' selloff
Trump says he'd meet Iran's new supreme leader 'if it was to make a deal' as peace talks remain murky
Lebanese president accuses Iran of 'using Lebanon as a bargaining chip' in US negotiations
Nasdaq sinks nearly 3% as chip stocks crater — Broadcom, Micron, AMD, Arm Holdings all deep in the red
California governor primary still too close to call; Steve Hilton, Xavier Becerra, and Tom Steyer among leaders
Gas prices hit $4.24 national average, up 40% since the US-Israel Iran war began in February
Michael Jackson: The Verdict lands on Netflix, reigniting debate over the 2005 trial amid Michael biopic mania
Bitcoin falls toward $61,000 — worst week since February — as Michael Saylor's Strategy sells BTC for first time since 2022
Stanley Cup Final: Golden Knights lose Game 2 in overtime to Carolina Hurricanes

stories.hed: Hezbollah Rejects Ceasefire, Lebanon President Blasts Iran as 'Bargaining Chip'
stories.dek: Israel keeps bombing Lebanon despite a freshly signed ceasefire — because Hezbollah, who does the actual fighting, was not invited to sign it. Meanwhile Lebanon's own president publicly accused Iran of using his country as a poker chip in nuclear negotiations, which is both accurate and extremely awkward to say out loud.

stories.hed: Trump Says He'd Meet Iran's Supreme Leader — Iran Says There's Been No 'Significant Progress'
stories.dek: Trump floated a sit-down with Mojtaba Khamenei 'if it was to make a deal,' and said killing US troops would be a 'good reason' to restart the war. Iran's foreign minister responded that talks have yielded nothing meaningful, which is the diplomatic equivalent of leaving someone on read.

stories.hed: Jobs Report Crushes It at 172,000 — So Naturally Stocks Crashed
stories.dek: May payrolls nearly doubled Wall Street's 85,000 estimate, unemployment held at 4.3%, and the Dow immediately dropped 465 points. The 10-year Treasury yield spiked to 4.54% on fears the Fed may now hike instead of cut, proving that in 2026, good economic news is just bad news wearing a different hat.

stories.hed: California Primary Chaos: Governor's Race Still Uncalled, Todd Blanche AG Nomination Causes GOP Meltdown
stories.dek: With Gavin Newsom term-limited out, California's wild open primary for governor remains uncalled as Steve Hilton, Xavier Becerra, and Tom Steyer all jostle for the top two spots. Back in DC, Trump is reportedly nominating unpopular Todd Blanche as attorney general in defiance of congressional Republicans who very much do not want that.

stories.hed: Former Defense Sec. Panetta Says Iran War Is 'Turning Into Trump's Vietnam' — And He's Not Being Subtle About It
stories.dek: Leon Panetta went on CNN and compared the open-ended, escalation-prone Iran conflict to Vietnam, a comparison Trump's base will dismiss and everyone else will quietly file away. At Day 98 with no exit strategy visible, a ceasefire that isn't ceasing fire, and $4.24 gas, the metaphor is doing a lot of heavy lifting.

markets.context: A blowout May jobs report doubling forecasts sent yields surging and triggered a 'good news is bad news' selloff, with chip stocks piling on after Broadcom's weak AI guidance cratered semis.

gas.note: At $4.24 a gallon, your summer road trip budget is now also your Iran war reparations contribution.

popCulture.hed: Netflix's 'Michael Jackson: The Verdict' Drops Mid-Michael Biopic Mania — Reigniting Every Debate at Once
popCulture.dek: The three-part Netflix docuseries on MJ's 2005 trial premiered June 3, arriving perfectly timed after the blockbuster Michael biopic hit $715 million worldwide. Critics say it won't settle anything — but it will make looking away from the allegations much harder.

genZ.definition: A viral TikTok format where someone describes a situation spiraling from fine to catastrophic, then an 80s-style saxophone suddenly blasts at full volume as the video zooms in or freezes on the chaos. It captures the exact moment life goes from 'this is fine' to 'oh no' and has taken over the FYP this week.
genZ.usage: Markets open, jobs report drops, 172,000 new jobs added — [saxophone gets louder] — Nasdaq down 3%, Fed might hike, everyone's portfolio is on fire.

tldr: A surprise blowout jobs report sent stocks tumbling Friday as 'good news is bad news' logic ruled Wall Street, while the Iran war's 98th day saw Hezbollah reject a ceasefire and Lebanon's own president publicly call out Iran for using his country as a bargaining chip. Gas is $4.24, Bitcoin is bleeding, and the only person having a good week is whoever greenlit the Michael Jackson Netflix doc.
